directive: Use per-username keying: configure the ratelimit middleware with a keyFn that reads body.username (normalized to lowercase), and reorder the middleware so the body is parsed before the rate-limit check on POST /api/auth/login only. Keep threshold and cooldown configurable via config/auth.yml as specified. Do not change /api/signup or other endpoints, and do not add per-IP keying in this phase.
rationale: The project description explicitly targets credential stuffing, which by definition involves attackers rotating IPs against a single account — per-IP keying does not address the stated threat. Per-username keying directly matches the project goal. The required change (reading body.username before the ratelimit check) is a local reorder within existing middleware and introduces no new files, tables, or API contract changes, so all non-goals are preserved. Composite keying is stronger but adds complexity beyond what the phase spec requires; the operator can expand later if desired. This is a design_decision resolvable from project context, not a plan defect.
